response.justification: The candidate is an experienced, versatile software developer with some C/C++ and embedded exposure, but is a poor match for this specific role. There is no evidence of native-level Japanese proficiency (a hard requirement), no Linux kernel investigation or modification experience, and no background in L2/L3 networking, switch products, or security technologies like MACsec or 802.1X. The career trajectory over the last decade centers on web/full-stack development (TypeScript, Node.js), far from embedded networking security firmware.
strengths: Solid C/C++ experience at National Instruments building IDE components for measurement software
Broad engineering background spanning web, mobile, desktop, and embedded systems with strong testing culture (TDD, craftsmanship)
Experience with low-latency, high-throughput systems programming (DLang, async I/O) showing systems-level competence
concerns: No indication of Japanese language ability, which is a required native-level qualification
No Linux kernel analysis/modification, LAN-switch, or L2/L3 networking protocol experience
No demonstrated security-domain expertise (MACsec, 802.1X, authentication systems, encryption, tamper detection)

key_factors.factor: General systems programming and C background
